title: AI Labs Watcher
subtitle: Public-source tracker of the six frontier AI labs — strategic posture, major moves, indicators, and market-position map. Weekly cadence after a Q1 2026 consolidated baseline.
scope: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google DeepMind, Meta AI, xAI, Mistral. Seeded from public materials only: press, earnings transcripts, product pages, public filings, official blog posts, and mainstream press coverage through April 2026.
objective: Track the strategic evolution of the six leading frontier AI labs so any reader at any period check-in sees current posture, the moves each lab made this period, live indicator values, predictions still pending, and where each lab sits on the research-vs-product and openness axes.
successCondition: A reader opening the doc at the end of any monitoring period knows each lab's current strategic posture and how it shifted, the discrete moves each lab made with intent and outcome, the latest indicator values with dated sources, and which labs moved on the position map.

objectiveFacets.name: Postures accurate
objectiveFacets.description: Each tracked lab's strategic posture reflects behaviour observed in the current period.

objectiveFacets.name: Moves logged
objectiveFacets.description: Every major public move by a tracked lab in the period is recorded with dateOf, intent, and outcome.

objectiveFacets.name: Predictions tracked
objectiveFacets.description: Every 'what comes next' claim has a live resolution status.

objectiveFacets.name: Indicators current
objectiveFacets.description: Tracked numeric indicators reflect the latest prints with as-of dates and sources.

objectiveFacets.name: Map current
objectiveFacets.description: Every tracked lab has a position on the map, and prior-period positions are preserved.

objectiveFacets.name: Period attribution
objectiveFacets.description: Every update is attributable to a named monitoring period.

periods.summary: Consolidated baseline as of mid-April 2026. Six labs placed from Q1–early-H1 public sources; initial moves logged; four predictions open. Weekly cadence from here — next periods 2026-W17, 2026-W18, ...

invariants.name: Posture must cite a signal
invariants.statement: Every lab card must cite at least one public signal (press, earnings, product page, major interview) dated in the current or prior period.

invariants.name: Every move has a dateOf and byCompany
invariants.statement: Every strategic-move-log card must carry a dateOf and a byCompany reference.

invariants.name: Every prediction carries a resolution status
invariants.statement: Every prediction rung must carry a resolution status with rationale.

invariants.name: Every indicator is dated
invariants.statement: Every indicator card must have a latestValue with an as-of date and a source.

invariants.name: Map covers every tracked lab
invariants.statement: The position map must include a card for every lab in the labs section.

invariants.name: Every source has a publication date
invariants.statement: Every citation card must carry a publishedAt field.

callouts.title: What this tracker is and isn't
items: Built entirely from public sources — press releases, earnings commentary, product pages, official blog posts, mainstream press coverage, public interviews. No confidential intel.
Postures are inferred from behaviour. Where a read is uncertain, the prose says so.
Figures are directional where no public precise number exists (revenue, GPU spend). Precise figures cite their source.

Weekly cadence: run /competitor-sync docs/ai-labs-watcher.json 2026-W17 each Monday; the doc accumulates rather than restarts. Slower topics (AI-and-labor, macro regimes) would run quarterly or half-yearly — cadence is a domain choice.

sections.title: Tracked labs
sections.rationale: Six frontier AI labs tracked by current strategic posture and how it evolves period over period.

data.name: OpenAI
data.currentBet: Aggressive product deployment across consumer (ChatGPT, Sora, operator-style agents) and enterprise (API, Teams, Enterprise), anchored to the GPT-5 family and the Stargate infrastructure partnership. Closed weights; Microsoft as hyperscaler partner; visible pivot toward building a full consumer OS-layer (Operator, memory, agentic workflows) rather than competing as a pure API provider.
activeInitiatives: GPT-5 family rollout across ChatGPT tiers and API
Operator agent and memory product
Stargate infrastructure buildout with Oracle/SoftBank/Microsoft
Enterprise/ChatGPT Teams motion scaling into the Fortune 1000

notes.text: OpenAI blog posts on GPT-5, Operator, and Stargate announcement through Q1 2026.

data.name: Anthropic
data.currentBet: Frontier model quality at the top (Claude Opus 4.7) with a tiered Sonnet/Haiku ladder, coupled with Claude Code CLI and the Model Context Protocol as an ecosystem play. Enterprise-first distribution, AWS as primary hyperscaler partner. Closed weights, but the ecosystem plumbing (MCP, Claude Code, the Agent SDK) is deliberately open for third parties to build on.
activeInitiatives: Claude Opus 4.7 with 1M-context release
Claude Code CLI and IDE integrations
Model Context Protocol adoption across tooling
Enterprise sales motion via AWS Bedrock + direct

notes.text: Anthropic blog posts on Claude Opus 4.x family, Claude Code, and MCP through April 2026.

data.name: Google DeepMind
data.sector: Frontier AI lab · integrated with tech-giant distribution
data.geography: London / Mountain View · global via Google products
data.strategicPosture: Integrated tech-giant lab running a dual-tier model strategy
data.currentBet: Gemini 3 frontier family deeply wired into Google's existing product surfaces (Search, Android, Workspace, Pixel), plus Gemma 4 — a separate open-weight family released April 2026 under Apache 2.0 spanning on-device to 31B dense. Google is the only frontier lab running closed-frontier and open-weight tiers simultaneously, with TPU economics as the structural subsidy that makes both viable. Agentic play via Astra; heavy investment in multi-modal and long-horizon reasoning. Research depth from DeepMind's legacy combined with Google-scale distribution advantages. Gemma 4 adds a second adoption surface — 10M base-model downloads in its first 7 days, 500M+ cumulative Gemma family, 100K+ community models — and national-government collaborations (Sarvam in India, AI Singapore for Southeast Asia) training sovereign-language models on top.
data.evolutionSinceLastPeriod: Refreshed 2026-04-21 to capture the Gemma 4 release (April 2) as a strategic posture update. Google's posture shifts from closed-frontier-via-product-funnel to explicit dual-tier (closed-frontier + open-weight). Original 2026-H1 capture remains accurate for the Gemini 3 / Astra / TPU story.
activeInitiatives: Gemini 3 rollout across Search, Android, Workspace
Astra agent across Pixel and Android
Workspace AI and Enterprise Gemini tier
TPU scale-out as capacity lever
Gemma 4 family (E2B, E4B, 26B MoE, 31B dense) under Apache 2.0
Per-Layer Embeddings (PLE) architecture for on-device deployment
Official vertical variants: ShieldGemma (guardrails), MedGemma (radiology / chest X-ray)
Android Studio offline agent mode powered by Gemma on-device
Community national-language variants (AI Singapore, Sarvam)

notes.text: Google and DeepMind blogs on Gemini 3, Astra, and integrated product launches through Q1 2026.

Omar Sanseviero (Google DeepMind), 'Gemma, DeepMind's Family of Open Models', AI Engineer conference 2026-04, https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_gVFUEdhCyI — primary source for Gemma 4 architecture, adoption numbers, and dual-tier framing.

data.name: Meta AI
data.sector: Frontier AI lab · consumer + open-weights ecosystem
data.currentBet: Continue shipping Llama family as the dominant open-weights frontier lineage, now with the Meta Superintelligence Labs push for frontier capability, while distributing AI across consumer surfaces (Instagram, WhatsApp, Ray-Ban/Orion glasses, AI Studio). Uses open-weight releases as both ecosystem strategy and talent/developer flywheel. Very high capex — GPU spend among the largest globally.
activeInitiatives: Meta Superintelligence Labs capability push
Llama 4 family open-weights release
Ray-Ban / Orion smart glasses AI integration
AI Studio and consumer-facing assistants

notes.text: Meta blog posts on Llama 4, Superintelligence Labs, and glasses/consumer AI through April 2026.

data.name: xAI
data.sector: Frontier AI lab · integrated with X and Tesla
data.currentBet: Grok as the AI surface across Musk's holdings — X social distribution, Tesla robotaxi/HUD tie-in, and the Colossus datacenter as a capex-driven capability play. Mix of closed-weights flagship models with periodic open-weight releases of older generations. The distribution advantage comes from X and Tesla reach; the capability advantage, when it shows, comes from the Memphis compute bet.
activeInitiatives: Grok 4 and subsequent frontier models
Colossus 2 datacenter expansion in Memphis
Grok integration across X premium and Tesla vehicles
Selective open-weights releases of prior generations

notes.text: xAI blog, Musk public commentary, and press coverage of Colossus buildout through Q1 2026.

data.name: Mistral
data.sector: Frontier AI lab · European open + enterprise
data.currentBet: Position as the European sovereign AI alternative — mostly open weights, enterprise-focused distribution, deep partnerships with EU governments and large European enterprises. Runs a two-track product line: open-weights (Mistral Large, Mixtral, Nemo) plus closed-weight enterprise tiers. Plays on regulatory proximity and data-sovereignty concerns that closed US labs can't easily address.
activeInitiatives: Mistral Large 2 and Mixtral updates
EU government and enterprise contracts
La Plateforme enterprise distribution
Sovereign-cloud deployments across the EU

notes.text: Mistral blog posts, French and EU press coverage, and enterprise partnership announcements through Q1 2026.

sections.title: Strategic moves this period
sections.rationale: Discrete actions taken by the tracked labs in 2026-H1 — launches, pricing changes, partnerships, infrastructure commitments. Events that happened; citations about them live in the sources section.

data.name: OpenAI announces Stargate infrastructure partnership
data.intent: Lock in multi-hundred-billion-dollar compute capacity through a joint venture with Oracle, SoftBank, and Microsoft, decoupling OpenAI's capacity ceiling from Microsoft Azure alone.
data.observedEffect: Repositions OpenAI from Azure-dependent to a consortium-scale infrastructure player. Effect on unit economics and competitor capacity pricing remains to be measured.

notes.text: Reuters and FT coverage of Stargate announcement, January 2026.

data.name: Anthropic ships Claude Opus 4.7 with 1M-context and expanded MCP ecosystem
data.intent: Push enterprise usage of long-context workflows and establish MCP as the default protocol for connecting external tools to frontier models, creating an ecosystem moat around Claude.
data.observedEffect: MCP adoption visible across third-party developer tools; enterprise API usage uptick. Too early to call on ecosystem lock-in.

notes.text: Anthropic blog posts on Claude Opus 4.7 and MCP through March 2026.

data.name: Meta launches Superintelligence Labs with aggressive hiring
data.intent: Catch up on frontier capability by consolidating senior AI talent under a dedicated organisation, separate from product-AI teams, with compensation packages reported well above industry norms.
data.observedEffect: Visible talent movement into Meta from OpenAI, Anthropic, and DeepMind; upward pressure on industry compensation bands. Frontier capability impact unmeasurable in this period.

notes.text: The Information and Bloomberg coverage of Meta Superintelligence Labs hiring, February–March 2026.

data.name: Google DeepMind ships Gemini 3 across product surfaces
data.intent: Bring a frontier-competitive model into Search, Workspace, Android, and Pixel simultaneously, leveraging distribution rather than standalone-product competition with OpenAI/Anthropic.
data.observedEffect: Gemini-powered features rolled across Google products; consumer Search experience meaningfully reshaped. Measurable impact on AI Overview usage still developing.

notes.text: Google blog announcements of Gemini 3 rollouts, February 2026.

data.name: Mistral announces sovereign-cloud deployments with two EU governments
data.intent: Convert regulatory tailwind from the EU AI Act and data-sovereignty concerns into government and regulated-industry contracts that US labs cannot bid on comparably.
data.observedEffect: Two EU national-government deployments announced; adds to the pipeline of EU-regulated enterprise pilots. Revenue impact still to materialise.

notes.text: Les Échos and Mistral press releases on sovereign-cloud deployments, March 2026.

data.name: Google ships Gemma 4 family — Apache 2.0 open-weight at scale
data.intent: Ship a full open-weight family (E2B, E4B, 26B MoE, 31B dense) under Apache 2.0 with day-zero multi-provider hosting and deep OSS-runtime integration. Position Google as the only frontier lab running closed-frontier and open-weight tiers simultaneously, using TPU economics as the structural subsidy. The PLE architecture on E-variants enables on-device deployment at phone / Raspberry Pi / laptop scale.
data.observedEffect: 10M Gemma 4 base-model downloads in the first 7 days; 500M+ cumulative Gemma family lifetime; 100K+ community models across the family, 1,000+ on Gemma 4 in week one. National-government sovereign-language programs (Sarvam in India, AI Singapore) training on top. Vertical variants (ShieldGemma, MedGemma) shipped alongside. Independent benchmark verification of 84.3% GPQA / 89.2% AIME claims pending through 2026-Q3.

notes.text: Omar Sanseviero (Google DeepMind) at AI Engineer 2026-04, https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_gVFUEdhCyI; Google Gemma 4 launch materials.

sections.title: What comes next
sections.rationale: Predictions about the frontier AI lab landscape through 2026–2027. Each rung resolves as evidence accumulates.

data.name: A frontier coding agent ships autonomous ticket-to-PR at ≥80% acceptance by end 2026

notes.text: Baseline: Claude Code and OpenAI Codex active in beta with ticket-working skills. Public benchmarks (SWE-bench Verified) climbing but not at autonomous-trust level yet.

Resolution criterion: if any frontier lab publishes a blog post demonstrating a deployed agent resolving live GitHub issues with ≥80% of PRs accepted by human maintainers without rework, confirmed. If all tracked labs are still at human-in-loop assistance by year-end 2026, refuted.

data.name: US federal AI regulation binding on frontier labs passes by end 2026

notes.text: Multiple bills in committee through 2025; Executive Branch signals mixed; state-level (CA, NY) activity proceeding independently.

Resolution criterion: passage of federal legislation (not an executive order) imposing specific compliance obligations on frontier-scale model developers. Confirmed if signed into law by 2026-12-31. Refuted otherwise.

data.name: At least one tracked lab exits frontier training (acquisition, pivot, or shutdown) by end 2027

notes.text: Historical pattern for capex-heavy technology races: the top 6 compresses to a top 3–4 over 3–5 years. Mistral and xAI have the most structural pressure given capital scale vs. OpenAI/DeepMind/Meta.

Resolution criterion: announcement of acquisition by a larger entity, a named pivot away from frontier-model training, or a shutdown of a tracked lab's frontier program by 2027-12-31. Confirmed on the first such event. Refuted if all six still operate independent frontier programs at year-end 2027.

data.name: EU AI Act causes at least one frontier lab to delay a major EU launch by end 2026

notes.text: AI Act provisions for general-purpose AI taking effect August 2026. Apple Intelligence's prior EU delay shows this pattern is real for AI features.

Resolution criterion: a tracked lab publicly announces a EU-specific launch delay or feature restriction citing the AI Act. Confirmed on first such announcement. Refuted if no delays announced by 2026-12-31.

sections.title: Indicator dashboard
sections.rationale: Numeric indicators tracked across periods. Values carried as text with source; trend is categorical (rising / stable / falling / resolved).

data.name: OpenAI annualised revenue (ARR, reported)
data.forecast: Press reporting suggests trajectory toward $20B+ by end 2026 if ChatGPT and enterprise scale as planned.

notes.text: The Information and Bloomberg revenue coverage, late 2025 / early 2026.

data.name: Anthropic annualised revenue (ARR, reported)
data.forecast: Fast enterprise growth; Claude Code and MCP ecosystem adoption may accelerate.

notes.text: The Information and Reuters coverage of Anthropic's 2026 revenue trajectory.

data.name: Combined frontier-lab capex commitments (announced, 2025–2027)
data.latestValue: $500B+ cumulative announced (Stargate + Meta + Google TPU + xAI Colossus combined)
data.forecast: Competitive pressure points toward further announcements through 2026–2027.

notes.text: Public announcements aggregated from Reuters, FT, Bloomberg Q1 2026.

data.name: Frontier SWE-bench Verified (best reported score)
data.latestValue: ~70% (reported on Claude Opus 4.x and GPT-5 family in early 2026)
data.forecast: Rising toward 80%+ plausible within the year if agentic scaffolding improves at current rate.

notes.text: SWE-bench Verified leaderboard and lab blog claims, early 2026.

data.name: Open-weights frontier gap (best open model vs. best closed model on GPQA)
data.latestValue: ~3 points behind frontier closed if Gemma 4 31B's reported 84.3% holds (vs. Opus 4.7 at ~87%); 5–8 points for Llama 4 / Mistral Large 2. Late-period Gemma 4 release is the gap-closer — independent verification pending.
data.asOf: 2026-04-21 · Gemma 4 claims April 2026, Llama / Mistral Q1 2026
data.forecast: Gap closes meaningfully if Gemma 4's 84.3% GPQA survives independent evaluation. Meta Superintelligence Labs push and DeepSeek next-gen are the parallel threads.

notes.text: Public benchmark reports from Hugging Face, artificial-analysis.ai, and lab blogs. Gemma 4 claims from Google launch materials April 2026 (Omar Sanseviero, AI Engineer).

data.name: Mistral announced enterprise / government deployments (cumulative)
data.latestValue: Growing pipeline — multiple EU government and large-enterprise partnerships announced through Q1 2026
data.forecast: Rising if EU AI Act enforcement drives data-sovereignty demand.

notes.text: Mistral press releases and Les Échos coverage, Q1 2026.

sections.title: Period notes
sections.rationale: One card per monitoring period summarising what shifted, what resolved, and what matters for next period.

notes.text: Consolidated baseline as of mid-April 2026. Six labs placed: OpenAI (product-led / closed), Anthropic (safety-forward enterprise / closed with open plumbing), Google DeepMind (integrated / closed), Meta AI (open-weights challenger), xAI (Grok-Tesla-X integration / mostly closed), Mistral (European open champion). Five consolidated strategic moves logged across Stargate, MCP/Opus-4.7, Superintelligence Labs, Gemini 3, and Mistral sovereign-cloud. Four predictions open. Six indicators dashboarded. No prior period for map comparison. Cadence shifts to weekly from 2026-W17.

notes.title: Focus for 2026-W17 and beyond
items: Does the SWE-bench frontier cross the 80% threshold and do any labs claim production-autonomous coding agents?
Does the EU AI Act enforcement window (August 2026 onward) produce a first public launch delay?
Does Meta Superintelligence Labs begin publishing frontier-competitive results, or does the hiring remain front-loaded without capability output?
Does OpenAI or Anthropic ARR print move the sector's revenue leaderboard materially?
Any M&A or capital-raise signal from Mistral or xAI that reshapes their structural position?
